data.name:
Texture sheet of sci-fi mechanical greeble panels, presented as a flat delit albedo map for a PBR material. Dense hard-surface detail: recessed rectangular vents, hex bolts, circular access ports, ribbed heat-sink strips, inset cable channels, hazard stripe decals, small indicator panels, stamped alphanumeric hull markings, flush rivets, interlocking plated sections. Painted industrial surfaces in desaturated gunmetal gray with accent panels in muted olive, faded rust-orange, and dusty cyan, plus small yellow-and-black hazard decals.
Rendered appearance: every recessed area is the exact same brightness as every raised area. A bolt head and the surface it sits on read as identical tone. The inside of a vent slot is the same value as the panel around it. Crevices are not darker. Edges are not brighter. The image looks wrong and unnatural to a human eye — it looks like a paint-by-numbers color chart, not a photograph. Imagine the panel was scanned with a cross-polarized filter that removes all specular response, then had ambient occlusion mathematically divided out, leaving only pigment. Color information exists; lighting information does not.
Style reference: Substance Designer base color output, Quixel Megascans delit albedo, texture atlas sheet, material ID map with real colors. Orthographic top-down, tileable, seamless wrap at all four edges.

data.name:
Orthographic top-down render of a sci-fi greeble panel material, tileable, seamless, PBR showcase shot. Clean gunmetal gray painted plating with muted olive and cyan accent panels, yellow-black hazard stripes, glowing cyan indicator LEDs. Dense detail: recessed vents, hex bolts, access ports, heat-sink ribs, cable channels, stamped hull markings, flush rivets. Factory-fresh surfaces, no weathering, no corrosion, no oxidation.
Full PBR response: sharp metallic reflections on bolts, anisotropic streaks on brushed strips, matte paint, deep ambient occlusion in every crevice and seam, soft contact shadows, emissive LED bloom spilling onto nearby metal. Soft overhead studio lighting with subtle directional bias for surface definition, cinematic contrast, slight HDR glow.
Square format, flat top-down view, Marmoset Toolbag material preview, Substance Designer render output.

data.name:
Texture sheet of sci-fi mechanical greeble panels, presented as a flat delit albedo map for a PBR material. Dense hard-surface detail: recessed rectangular vents, hex bolts, circular access ports, ribbed heat-sink strips, inset cable channels, hazard stripe decals, small indicator panels, stamped alphanumeric hull markings, flush rivets, interlocking plated sections. Painted industrial surfaces in desaturated gunmetal gray with accent panels in muted olive, faded rust-orange, and dusty cyan, plus small yellow-and-black hazard decals.
Rendered appearance: every recessed area is the exact same brightness as every raised area. A bolt head and the surface it sits on read as identical tone. The inside of a vent slot is the same value as the panel around it. Crevices are not darker. Edges are not brighter. The image looks wrong and unnatural to a human eye — it looks like a paint-by-numbers color chart, not a photograph. Imagine the panel was scanned with a cross-polarized filter that removes all specular response, then had ambient occlusion mathematically divided out, leaving only pigment. Color information exists; lighting information does not.
Style reference: Substance Designer base color output, Quixel Megascans delit albedo, texture atlas sheet, material ID map with real colors. Orthographic top-down, tileable, seamless wrap at all four edges.
